_comment: ProveIt bottling fault scenarios. Simulated scenarios carry their own deterministic answer-card content (honest: simulated asset, no OEM manual). Live Conv_Simple scenarios reuse the REAL card from demo/conv_simple_demo.py (use_conv_simple_card) or a GS10-fault builder that cites the real GS10 evidence. evidence_refs are ids in demo/evidence/evidence_manifest.json.

scenarios.question: Why did the filler stop and bottles back up?

card.most_likely_cause: A bottle jam at the filler infeed — not a supply or pressure problem.
why: Jam-detected is TRUE at the filler
Bottles-per-minute dropped to 0 and infeed accumulation is full
Tank level and bowl pressure are nominal — supply is fine
technician_checks: Clear the jammed bottle(s) at the filler infeed star/guide.
Confirm the jam sensor clears after the jam is removed.
Verify infeed conveyor/timing screw is feeding single-file.
human_review: filler01 is a SIMULATED asset — no OEM manual on file (deterministic scenario).

scenarios.review_notes: Simulated. Demonstrates a discrete-MES jam (no real equipment).

scenarios.question: Why are capped bottles being rejected?

card.most_likely_cause: Cap application torque is below target (worn clutch / pads) — a quality fault, not a feed fault.
why: Cap torque is 9 in-lb vs a 12 in-lb target, with high variance
Cap chute level is OK and the capper is running — caps are available
technician_checks: Check the capping head clutch / friction pads for wear.
Re-verify the torque setpoint and re-run a torque audit.
Inspect caps for the correct size/lot.
human_review: capper01 is a SIMULATED asset — no OEM manual on file (deterministic scenario).

scenarios.question: Why did the conveyor stop?
scenarios.review_notes: LIVE supervised bench (Conv_Simple). Reuses the real demo/conv_simple_demo.py answer card — real GS10/Micro820 manuals + the PE-101 procedure.

scenarios.question: Why did the conveyor stop with a drive fault?
scenarios.review_notes: LIVE supervised bench. GS10 reports a fault code — this IS a drive fault (contrast with pe101_blocked, where the GS10 was healthy).
